name: George Cables
abstract: George Andrew Cables (born November 14, 1944) is an American jazz pianist and composer.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Freddie Hubbard Sextet, the Sonny Rollins Quartet, and the McCoy Tyner Quintet

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Marshall
about_quotes.evidence: we were like a house trio there. And there was no reason to leave then because all the guys that I would be working for in New York, they were all coming here. And so James Leary was the bass player. And so that lasted for five years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Marshall interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 8, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Hutcherson
about_quotes.evidence: I started doing a lot of things with George Cables. ... George Cables, we started doing a lot of stuff with George, because George was writing so much. ... We were just the same – we were like brothers.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Hutcherson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Monk Montgomery
about_quotes.evidence: We went back to Indianapolis and we worked that job, with Buddy and I and Eddie Moore and George Cables, the quartet.

about_quotes.speaker: Curtis Fuller
about_quotes.evidence: The drummer was Lenny White Jr. The bass player was Stanley Clarke and George Cables was the piano player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Curtis Fuller — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Winard Harper
about_quotes.evidence: one of the bands I had later on I remember I had George Cables play with me for quite some time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Winard Harper interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, October 10, 2014

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: We played at Danny’s Hideaway during this loft period, on a Sunday afternoon, double rhythm section: Moses and Lenny, Lanny Fields and Holland, George Cables, and me and Steve.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
